D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Quarter-Wave Anti-Reflection Coating

tune a thin film so its two reflections cancel and the lens stops glaring

A film a quarter-wavelength thick makes the reflection off its top arrive half a cycle behind the reflection off the substrate, so they cancel. Perfect cancellation needs n_film = √(n_air·n_sub).

Set the coating index, its thickness, and the substrate index. The curve is reflectance across the visible band; the dashed line is bare glass. The gold readout reports the minimum reflectance and its wavelength.
